prose: Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', exact four-center integrals, no density fitting) in PySCF, in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The cation (N-1) state used charge +1, mult 2; the anion (N+1) state used charge -1, mult 2. Geometry: user-supplied .xyz, used as-is (single-point on fixed geometry, not re-optimized at DFT). Partial charges from Mulliken scheme (PySCF). All three SCF jobs converged. Fukui charge conservation checks both passed (sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000). integrity.trustworthy=true. No warnings reported. Headline value: most electrophilic site is atom 3 (carbonyl carbon), f+ = 0.39938968. Most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (oxygen), f- = 0.23839906. These results are physically consistent with the known electrophilicity of the aldehyde carbonyl carbon and the nucleophilicity of the carbonyl oxygen in benzaldehyde.
